name: brainrender

metadata.name: brainrender
metadata.summary: Visualisation and exploration of brain atlases and other anatomical data

documentation: # brainrender *A user-friendly python library to create high-quality, 3D neuro-anatomical renderings combining data from publicly available brain atlases with user-generated experimental data.* [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/brainrender.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/brainrender) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/brainrender.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/brainrender) [![tests](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender/workflows/tests/badge.svg)](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender/actions) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/brainglobe/brainrender/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/brainglobe/brainrender) [![Downloads](https://static.pepy.tech/badge/brainrender)](https://pepy.tech/project/brainrender)     ![Example gallery](https://iiif.elifesciences.org/lax/65751%2Felife-65751-fig3-v3.tif/full/,1500/0/default.jpg) From: Claudi et al. (2021) Visualizing anatomically registered data with brainrender. eLife ## Documentation brainrender is a project of the BrainGlobe Initiative, which is a collaborative effort to develop a suite of Python-based software tools for computational neuroanatomy. A comprehensive online documentation for brainrender can be found on the BrainGlobe website [here](https://brainglobe.info/documentation/brainrender/index.html). Furthermore, an open-access journal article describing BrainRender has been published in eLife, available [here](https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.65751). ## Installation From PyPI: ``` pip install brainrender ``` ## Quickstart ``` python import random import numpy as np from brainrender import Scene from brainrender.actors import Points def get_n_random_points_in_region(region, N): """ Gets N random points inside (or on the surface) of a mesh """ region_bounds = region.mesh.bounds() X = np.random.randint(region_bounds[0], region_bounds[1], size=10000) Y = np.random.randint(region_bounds[2], region_bounds[3], size=10000) Z = np.random.randint(region_bounds[4], region_bounds[5], size=10000) pts = [[x, y, z] for x, y, z in zip(X, Y, Z)] ipts = region.mesh.inside_points(pts).coordinates return np.vstack(random.choices(ipts, k=N)) # Display the Allen Brain mouse atlas. scene = Scene(atlas_name="allen_mouse_25um", title="Cells in primary visual cortex") # Display a brain region primary_visual = scene.add_brain_region("VISp", alpha=0.2) # Get a numpy array with (fake) coordinates of some labelled cells coordinates = get_n_random_points_in_region(primary_visual, 2000) # Create a Points actor cells = Points(coordinates) # Add to scene scene.add(cells) # Add label to the brain region scene.add_label(primary_visual, "Primary visual cortex") # Display the figure. scene.render() ``` ## Seeking help or contributing We are always happy to help users of our tools, and welcome any contributions. If you would like to get in contact with us for any reason, please see the [contact page of our website](https://brainglobe.info/contact.html). ## Citing brainrender If you use brainrender in your scientific work, please cite: ``` Claudi, F., Tyson, A. L., Petrucco, L., Margrie, T.W., Portugues, R., Branco, T. (2021) "Visualizing anatomically registered data with Brainrender" eLife 2021;10:e65751 [doi.org/10.7554/eLife.65751](https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.65751) ``` BibTeX: ``` bibtex @article{Claudi2021, author = {Claudi, Federico and Tyson, Adam L. and Petrucco, Luigi and Margrie, Troy W. and Portugues, Ruben and Branco, Tiago}, doi = {10.7554/eLife.65751}, issn = {2050084X}, journal = {eLife}, pages = {1--16}, pmid = {33739286}, title = {{Visualizing anatomically registered data with brainrender}}, volume = {10}, year = {2021} } ```

If the list has length > 1 returns the list

functions.name: brainrender.actors.ruler.ruler
documentation:
Creates a ruler showing the distance between two points.
The ruler is composed of a line between the points and
a text indicating the distance.

```
param p1

list, np.ndarray with coordinates of first point

param p2

list, np.ndarray with coordinates of second point

param unit_scale

float. To scale the units (e.g. show mm instead of µm)

param units

str, name of unit (e.g. 'mm')

param s

float size of text
```

functions.name: brainrender.actors.ruler.ruler_from_surface
documentation:
Creates a ruler between a point and the brain's surface
\:param p1: list, np.ndarray with coordinates of point
\:param root: mesh or actor with brain's root
\:param axis: int, index of axis along which distance is computed
\:param unit_scale: float. To scale the units (e.g. show mm instead of µm)
\:param units: str, name of unit (e.g. 'mm')
\:param s: float size of text

functions.name: brainrender.actor.make_actor_label
documentation:
Adds a 2D text anchored to a point on the actor's mesh
to label what the actor is

```
param kwargs

keyword arguments can be passed to determine
text appearance and location:

source:6: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

size: int, text size. Default 300

color: str, text color. A list of colors can be passed

if None a gray color is used. Default None.

xoffset, yoffset, zoffset: integers that shift the label position

radius: radius of sphere used to denote label anchor. Set to 0 or None to hide.
```

methods.name: brainrender.actor.Actor.__init__
documentation:
Actor class representing anythng shown in a brainrender scene.
Methods in brainrender.actors are used to creates actors specific
for different data types.

An actor has a mesh, a name and a brainrender class type.
It also has methods to create a silhouette or a label.

```
param mesh

instance of vedo.Mesh

param name

str, actor name

param br_class

str, name of brainrender actors class

param is_text

bool, is it a 2d text or annotation?

param color

str, name or hex code of color to assign to actor's mesh

param alpha

float, transparency to assign to actor's mesh
```

methods.name: brainrender.actor.Actor.__getattr__
documentation:
If an unknown attribute is called, try `self.mesh.attr`
to get the mesh's attribute

documentation: Returns the coordinates of the mesh's center

documentation: Make an actor from a given mesh

documentation:
Create a new Actor with a sphere and a text

methods.name: brainrender.actor.Actor.make_silhouette
documentation: Create a new silhouette actor outlining this actor

methods.name: brainrender.actor.Actor.mirror
documentation:
Mirror the actor's mesh around the specified axis, using the
parent_center as the center of the mirroring operation. The axes can
be specified using an abbreviation, e.g. 'x' for the x-axis, or anatomical
convention e.g. 'sagittal'. If an atlas is provided, then the anatomical
space of the atlas is used, otherwise `asr` is assumed.

```
param axis

str, axis around which to mirror the mesh

param origin

np.ndarray, center of the mirroring operation

param atlas

BrainGlobeAtlas, atlas object to use for anatomical space
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.VideoMaker.__init__
documentation:
Creates a video by animating a scene and saving a sequence
of screenshots.

```
param scene

the instance of Scene to be animated

param save_fld

str, Path. Where the video will be savd

param save_name

str, name of the video

param fmt

str. Video format (e.g. 'mp4')

param make_frame_func

None, optional. If passed it should be a
function that takes the Scene to be animated as the fist argument abd
the current frame number as second. At every frame this function
can do what's needed to animate the scene

param size

str, size of video's frames in pixels
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.VideoMaker._make_frame
documentation:
Default `make_frame_func`. Rotates the camera in 3 directions

```
param scene

scene to be animated.

param frame_number

int, not used

param tot_frames

int, total number of frames

param resetcam

bool, if True the camera is reset

param azimuth

integer, specify the rotation in degrees
per frame on the relative axis. (Default value = 0)

param elevation

integer, specify the rotation in degrees
per frame on the relative axis. (Default value = 0)

param roll

integer, specify the rotation in degrees
per frame on the relative axis. (Default value = 0)
```

classes.name: brainrender.video.Animation

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation.__init__
documentation:
The animation class facilitates the creation of videos
by specifying a series of keyframes at given moments during
the video. At each keyframe various parameters (e.g. camera position)
is specified and the video is created by interpolating
between consecutive key frames.

```
param scene

the instance of Scene to be animated

param save_fld

str, Path. Where the video will be savd

param save_name

str, name of the video

param fmt

str. Video format (e.g. 'mp4')
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation.add_keyframe
documentation:
Add a keyframe to the video.

```
param time

float, time in seconds during the video
at which the keyframe takes place.

param duration

float, if >0 the key frame is repeated
every 5ms to go from start to start+duration

param zoom

camera zoom

param camera

dictionary of camera parameters

param interpol

str, if  or  specifies
the interpolation mode between key frames.

param callback

function which takes scene, current video
frame and total number of frames in video as arguments.
can be used to make stuff happen during a key frame (e.g. remove
an actor)
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation.get_keyframe_framenumber
documentation:
Keyframes are defines in units of time (s), so we need
to know to which frame each keyframe corresponds

```
param fps

int, frame rate
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation.generate_frames
documentation:
Loop to generate frames

```
param fps

int, frame rate

param duration

float, video duration in seconds

param video

vedo Video class used to create the video

param resetcam

bool, if True the camera is reset
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation.get_frame_params
documentation:
Get current parameters (e.g. camera position)
based on frame number and defined key frames.

If frame number is a keyframe or is after a keyframe
then the params are those of that/the last keyframe.
Else the params of two consecutive keyframes are interpolate
using either a linear or sigmoid function.

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation._make_frame
documentation:
Creates a frame with the correct params
and calls the keyframe callback function if defined.

```
param frame_number

int, current frame number
```

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation._interpolate_cameras
documentation: Interpolate the parameters of two cameras

methods.name: brainrender.video.Animation._interpolate_values
documentation: Interpolate two values

The animation class facilitates the creation of videos
by specifying a series of keyframes at given moments during
the video. At each keyframe various parameters (e.g. camera position)
is specified and the video is created by interpolating
between consecutive key frames.

modules.name: brainrender.scene
documentation:
Scene
    - Create a scene, add root and inset if necessary
    - add actor method
    - special methods

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene.__init__
documentation:
Main scene in brainrender.
It coordinates what should be rendered and how should it look like.

```
param root

bool. If true the brain root mesh is added

param atlas_name

str, name of the brainglobe atlas to be used

param check_latest

bool, if True checks that the atlas is the latest version

param inset

bool. If true an inset is shown with the brain's outline

param title

str. If true a title is added to the top of the window

param screenshots_folder

str, Path. Where the screenshots will be saved
```

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene._get_inset
documentation: Creates a small inset showing the brain's orientation

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene.add
documentation:
General method to add Actors to the scene.

```
param items

vedo.Mesh, Actor, (str, Path).
If str/path it should be a path to a .obj or .stl file.
Whatever the input it's turned into an instance of Actor
before adding it to the scne

param names

names to be assigned to the Actors

param classes

br_classes to be assigned to the Actors

param **kwargs

source:10: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

parameters to be passed to the individual
loading functions (e.g. to load from file and specify the color)
```

Dedicated method to add brain regions to render

str.
- if "both" the complete mesh is returned
- if "left"/"right" only the corresponding half

source:11: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

of the mesh is returned

bool. If true force adding of region even
if already rendered

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene.slice
documentation:
Slices actors with a plane.

```
param plane

str, Plane. If a string it needs to be
a supported plane from brainglobe's atlas api (e.g. 'frontal')
otherwise it should be a vedo.Plane mesh

param actors

list of actors to be sliced. If None all actors
will be sliced

param close_actors

If true the openings in the actors meshes
caused by the cut will be closed.

param invert

Invert the slice direction.
```

documentation: Prints an overview of the Actors in the scene.

documentation: Returns the meshes for all actors.

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene.clean_actors
documentation: returns only actors that are not Text objects and similar

methods.name: brainrender.scene.Scene.clean_renderables
documentation:
Returns meshes only for 'clean actors' (i.e. not text).
_mesh is returned to account for internal rotations.

methods.name: brainrender.actors.volume.Volume.__init__
documentation:
Takes a 3d numpy array with volumetric data
and returns an Actor with mesh: vedo.Volume.isosurface or a vedo.Volume.
BY default the volume is represented as a surface

To extract the surface:

: The isosurface needs a lower bound threshold, this can be
  either a user defined hard value (min_value) or the value
  corresponding to some percentile of the grid data.

```
param griddata

np.ndarray, 3d array with grid data. Can also be a vedo Volume
or a file path pointing to a .npy file

param griddata

np.ndarray, 3d array with grid data

param voxel_size

int, size of each voxel in microns

param min_quantile

float, percentile for threshold

param min_value

float, value for threshold

param cmap

str, name of colormap to use
```

:param as_surface, bool. default True. If True

: a surface mesh is returned instead of the whole volume

```
param volume_kwargs

keyword arguments for vedo's Volume class
```

documentation: Creates a vedo.Volume actor from a 3D numpy array with volume data.

methods.name: brainrender.actors.volume.Volume._from_file

methods.name: brainrender.actors.points.PointsDensity.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Volume actor showing the 3d density of a set
of points.

```
param data

np.ndarray, Nx3 array with cell coordinates

param colors

str, matplotlib colormap
```

from vedo:

: Generate a density field from a point cloud. Input can also be a set of 3D coordinates.
  Output is a `Volume`.
  The local neighborhood is specified as the `radius` around each sample position (each voxel).
  The density is expressed as the number of counts in the radius search.

  ```
  param int,list dims

  number of voxels in x, y and z of the output Volume.
  ```

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.streamlines.get_streamlines_data
documentation:
Given a list of expeirmental IDs, it downloads the streamline data
from the <https://neuroinformatics.nl> cache and saves them as
json files.

```
param eids

list of integers with experiments IDs
```

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.streamlines.get_streamlines_for_region
documentation:
Using the Allen Mouse Connectivity data and corresponding API, this function finds experiments whose injections
were targeted to the region of interest and downloads the corresponding streamlines data. By default, experiments
are selected for only WT mice and only when the region was the primary injection target.

```
param region

str with region to use for research
```

```
param gene

int, the gene_id for the gene being downloaded.
```

methods.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.api.GeneExpressionAPI.get_gene_data
documentation: Given a list of gene ids

methods.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.api.GeneExpressionAPI.griddata_to_volume
documentation:
Takes a 3d numpy array with volumetric gene expression
and returns a vedo.Volume.isosurface actor.
The isosurface needs a lower bound threshold, this can be
either a user defined hard value (min_value) or the value
corresponding to some percentile of the gene expression data.

```
param griddata

np.ndarray, 3d array with gene expression data

param min_quantile

float, percentile for threshold

param min_value

float, value for threshold
```

class_variables.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.api.GeneExpressionAPI.voxel_size
documentation: um

class_variables.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.api.GeneExpressionAPI.grid_size
documentation: number of voxels along each direction

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.ge_utils.check_gene_cached
documentation:
A gene is saved in a folder in cache_folder
with gene_id-exp_id as name. If the folder doesn't
exist the gene is not cached.

```
param cache_folder

str, path to general cache folder for all data

param gene_id

str name of gene

param exp_id

id of experiment
```

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.ge_utils.download_and_cache
documentation:
Given a url to download a gene's ISH experiment data,
this function download and unzips the data

```
param url

str, utl to download data

param cachedir

str, path to folder where data will be downloaded
```

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.ge_utils.load_cached_gene
documentation: Loads a gene's data from cache

functions.name: brainrender.atlas_specific.allen_brain_atlas.gene_expression.ge_utils.read_raw
documentation:
reads a .raw file with gene expression data
downloaded from the Allen atlas and returns
a numpy array with the correct grid_size.
See as reference:

> <http://help.brain-map.org/display/mousebrain/API#API-Expression3DGridsz>

```
param filepath

str or Path object
```

variables.name: brainrender.settings.DEBUG
documentation: set to True to see more detailed logs

variables.name: brainrender.settings.vsettings.screenshot_transparent_background
documentation: vedo for transparent bg

variables.name: brainrender.settings.DEFAULT_ATLAS
documentation: default atlas

variables.name: brainrender.settings.DEFAULT_CAMERA
documentation: Default camera settings (orientation etc. see brainrender.camera.py)

variables.name: brainrender.settings.INTERACTIVE
documentation: rendering interactive ?

variables.name: brainrender.settings.LW
documentation: e.g. for silhouettes

variables.name: brainrender.settings.ROOT_COLOR
documentation: color of the overall brain model's actor

variables.name: brainrender.settings.ROOT_ALPHA
documentation: transparency of the overall brain model's actor'

variables.name: brainrender.settings.SHADER_STYLE
documentation: affects the look of rendered brain regions: [metallic, plastic, shiny, glossy, cartoon]

variables.name: brainrender.settings.WHOLE_SCREEN
documentation: If true render window is full screen

methods.name: brainrender.atlas.Atlas.__init__
documentation:
Brainrender's Atlas class subclasses BrainGlobeAtlas
to add methods to get regions meshes as Actors
and to get a plane at a given point and normal.

```
param atlas_name

str, atlas name from brainglobe's atlas API atlases

param check_latest

bool, if True checks that the atlas is the latest version
```

methods.name: brainrender.atlas.Atlas.zoom
documentation: Returns the best camera zoom given the atlas resolution

methods.name: brainrender.atlas.Atlas._get_region_color
documentation: Gets the rgb color of a region in the atlas

methods.name: brainrender.atlas.Atlas.get_region
documentation:
Get brain regions meshes as Actors
\:param regions: str with names of brain regions in the atlas
\:param alpha: float
\:param color: str

methods.name: brainrender.atlas.Atlas.get_plane
documentation:
Returns a plane going through a point at pos, oriented
orthogonally to the vector norm and of width and height
sx, sy.

```
param pos

3-tuple or list with x,y,z, coords of point the plane goes through

param norm

3-tuple with plane's normal vector (optional)

param sx, sy

int, width and height of the plane

param plane

"sagittal", "horizontal", or "frontal"

param color, alpha

plane color and transparency
```

functions.name: brainrender.camera.get_camera
documentation:
Returns the parameters for a pre-defined camera

```
param camera

str
```

functions.name: brainrender.camera.check_camera_param
documentation:
Check that a dictionary of camera parameters
is complete. Must have entries:
["pos", "viewup", "clipping_range"]

```
param camera

str, dict
```

functions.name: brainrender.camera.set_camera_params
documentation:
Set camera parameters
\:param camera: camera obj
\:param params: dictionary of camera parameters

functions.name: brainrender.camera.set_camera
documentation:
Sets the position of the camera of a brainrender scene.

```
param scene

instance of Scene

param camera

either a string with the name of one of the pre-defined cameras, or
a dictionary of camera parameters.
```

functions.name: brainrender.camera.get_camera_params
documentation:
Given an active brainrender scene or a camera, it return
the camera parameters.

```
param scene

instance of Scene whose camera is to be used

param camera

camera obj
```

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render.__init__
documentation:
Backend for Scene, handles all rendering and exporting
related tasks.

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render._get_plotter
documentation:
Make a vedo plotter with
fancy axes and all

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render._make_axes
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with axes
parameters for the vedo plotter

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render._prepare_actor
documentation:
When an actor is first rendered, a transform matrix
is applied to its points to correct axes orientation
mismatches: https://github.com/brainglobe/brainglobe-atlasapi/issues/73

Once an actor is 'corrected' it spawns labels and silhouettes as needed

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render._apply_style
documentation: Sets the rendering style for each mesh

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render.render
documentation:
Renders the scene.

```
param interactive

bool. If note settings.INTERACTIVE is used.
If true the program's execution is stopped and users
can interact with scene.

param camera

str, dict. If none the default camera is used.
Pass a valid camera input to specify the camera position when
the scene is rendered.

param zoom

float, if None atlas default is used

param resetcam

bool, if True the camera is reset between renders

param kwargs

additional arguments to pass to self.plotter.show
```

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render.export
documentation:
Exports the scene to a .html
file for online renderings.

```
param savepath

str, Path to a .html file to save the export
```

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render.screenshot
documentation:
Takes a screenshot of the current view
and save it to file.
Screenshots are saved in `screenshots_folder`
(see Scene)

```
param name

str, name of png file

param scale

float, >1 for higher resolution
```

methods.name: brainrender.render.Render.keypress
documentation:
Handles key presses for interactive view
-s: take's a screenshot
-q: closes the window
-c: prints the current camera parameters
